name: Reeflex Demo 5 - Watch Before You Enforce (observe posture)

nodes.name: When clicking 'Execute workflow'
nodes.notes: Stand-in for whatever actually triggers this (a schedule, a webhook, an agent tool call).

nodes.name: Two would-be-blocked actions (observe posture sample data)
nodes.notes: Stand-in for your real upstream data. Replace with the real actions you want to shadow-test a policy change against before enforcing it.

nodes.name: Reeflex Gate
nodes.notes: reeflex-core has no server-side 'observe mode' -- every call here is a normal, real /v1/decide call and gets a real verdict (this is NOT a mock). What makes this demo 'observe' is entirely what THIS WORKFLOW does with the verdict afterwards: it logs it and proceeds anyway instead of branching to a block/hold path. See demo5-README.md.

nodes.name: Audit: would-be HOLD logged (does not block)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real logging/metrics node (write {{$json.reeflex.reason}}, {{$json.reeflex.rule}} to your SIEM/log store). In an ENFORCE posture this is where the workflow would stop and wait for a human. In this OBSERVE posture it is a log line, then the workflow keeps going -- see the next node.

nodes.name: Audit: would-be DENY logged (does not block)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - replace with a real logging/metrics node (write {{$json.reeflex.reason}}, {{$json.reeflex.rule}} to your SIEM/log store). In an ENFORCE posture this action would be terminally blocked (see demo4). In this OBSERVE posture it is a log line, then the workflow keeps going -- see the next node.

nodes.name: Proceed (action runs regardless of verdict - OBSERVE POSTURE)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER - not a real action. All three Reeflex Gate outputs (Allowed directly, Held via the audit log, Denied via the audit log) converge here and the workflow proceeds -- this is the entire point of 'observe': you get the same real verdicts as enforce posture, logged, but the workflow never blocks on them. This is a WORKFLOW CHOICE (which output wires do), not a reeflex-core mode -- core does not know or care that this workflow ignores its verdict.

main.node: Two would-be-blocked actions (observe posture sample data)
Proceed (action runs regardless of verdict - OBSERVE POSTURE)
Audit: would-be HOLD logged (does not block)
Audit: would-be DENY logged (does not block)
Proceed (action runs regardless of verdict - OBSERVE POSTURE)
Proceed (action runs regardless of verdict - OBSERVE POSTURE)

meta.description: Reeflex n8n demo 5/5 (examples/n8n/, see top-level README.md for the index + credential setup). Teaches: 'observe' is a WORKFLOW POSTURE, not a reeflex-core mode -- the Gate node returns a real verdict either way, an audit/log node records what WOULD have happened (a would-be HOLD or DENY), and the workflow proceeds regardless, never blocking. Two sample actions (one would-be R2 hold, one would-be R3 deny), both logged, both proceed. Fully works against the live api-dev eval endpoint -- see demo5-README.md.
